overall.description: Cinematic game engine footage from Red Dead Redemption 2 showing an eagle flying over a geothermal basin with colorful hot springs, following a railway line across a rusted bridge, and navigating through a steep canyon with cascading waterfalls in a mountainous wilderness area.

scene_state.functional_zone: geothermal basin with colorful mineral hot springs

agents.ego_description: large eagle gliding over geothermal hot springs, surveying the vibrant blue and yellow mineral pools below

entered.detail: large circular hot spring with vivid turquoise water and yellow mineral crust
smaller hot spring with blue water visible in the distance
snow-capped rocky mountains forming the backdrop
geothermal vent releasing white steam into the air
yellow and orange mineral crust surrounding the hot springs

segments.scene_description: The eagle soars over a geothermal basin featuring vibrant blue hot springs ringed with yellow mineral deposits. Steam rises from geothermal vents scattered across the rocky terrain, with pine forests and distant mountains visible under a clear midday sky.

events.description: leaving geothermal basin behind, entering forested grassland
eagle adjusts flight path to align with distant railway track

agents.ego_description: eagle flying over rolling grassy hills and pine forest, adjusting course toward a railway line visible in the distance

entered.detail: rolling green hills covered in grass and scattered wildflowers
single railway track cutting through the landscape
rusted metal railway bridge visible in the far distance spanning a canyon

segments.scene_description: Leaving the geothermal area, the eagle flies over rolling grassy hills dotted with pine trees. A railway track comes into view in the distance, leading toward a bridge spanning a canyon, while the mountain range shifts to the left as the bird turns.

events.description: approaching and beginning to cross the railway bridge
eagle banks right to follow the canyon wall after crossing bridge

agents.ego_description: eagle flying low along railway track, crossing a rusted metal truss bridge and entering a steep-walled canyon

entered.detail: steep rocky cliff face with sparse vegetation
steep rocky cliff face dropping into the canyon
river flowing at the bottom of the deep canyon

segments.scene_description: The eagle follows the railway line across a rusted metal bridge spanning a deep canyon. Steep rocky walls rise on either side as the bird glides into the canyon, with a river visible far below, and the bridge disappears behind as the bird banks right.

events.description: entering the waterfall canyon area with tall cascades
flying through mist rising from the waterfalls

agents.ego_description: eagle flying through narrow canyon, navigating past cascading waterfalls and rising mist

entered.detail: tall cascading waterfall dropping down the canyon wall
multi-tiered waterfall with white water crashing down
white mist rising from the base of the waterfalls

segments.scene_description: The eagle descends into a narrow canyon featuring multiple cascading waterfalls. Mist rises from the falling water as the bird navigates between the steep rocky walls that now loom close on both sides, with dense pine trees clinging to the cliff faces.

merged_segments.scene_description: The shot follows a continuous aerial journey that begins over a vibrant geothermal basin and descends into the misty depths of a rugged canyon. It begins with an eagle soaring above bright blue hot springs and steaming vents set against a backdrop of distant mountains and pine forests. As the bird leaves the geothermal area, the landscape shifts to rolling grassy hills before a railway track emerges, leading the camera toward a rusted metal bridge. The scene then transitions from the open heights into a deep gorge as the eagle follows the tracks across the bridge and banks right, leaving the structure behind to glide between steep rocky walls. Finally, the camera descends further into a narrow canyon, navigating through rising mist and cascading waterfalls where dense pine trees cling to the towering cliffs.
